Start a build
How I work

I try to remove ambiguity before I remove bugs.

Most engineering pain starts earlier than the code. It starts when no one has agreed on the source of truth, the failure modes, or the exact problem being solved. My process is designed to force clarity before the project gets expensive.

Solo by design

You speak directly to the person doing the architecture, implementation, and technical tradeoff decisions.

Clear boundaries

I define what is in scope, what needs hardening, and what can wait so the work does not collapse into vagueness.

Step 1 I define the operating reality.

I identify the people, systems, and state transitions involved before reaching for implementation detail.

Step 2 I shape the architecture around risk.

If timing, reconciliation, or auditability can damage trust later, those concerns become first-class design inputs.

Step 3 I build in narrow increments.

That keeps the system understandable and makes it easier to catch drift, hidden assumptions, and unnecessary complexity.

Step 4 I finish with hardening, not hand-waving.

I care about how the product behaves when a dependency is slow, a value is stale, or an operator needs a straight answer.

Patch plans

I define the acceptance criteria and regression concerns before a change touches production behavior.

Automation where it helps

I use tooling, tests, and AI assistance pragmatically, but the judgment and accountability stay human.

Hardening focus

Time normalization, staleness checks, and data integrity are designed into the work, not bolted on later.

SS Developers

I build trading, fintech, and data-heavy systems for teams that need the software to stay correct when the environment gets messy.

© 2026 SS Developers. Built in Cape Town.
